text: DFS Pattern: Tree traversal (preorder, inorder, postorder). Preorder: process node before children (root-left-right). Inorder: left-root-right (gives sorted order for BST). Postorder: left-right-root (use when children must be processed before parent). All: O(n) time, O(h) space where h=height. Use recursion or explicit stack.

DFS Pattern: Path finding in graph/grid. DFS explores one path fully before backtracking. Use when you need to find any path, all paths, or existence of a path. Template: def dfs(node, visited): if node==target: return True; visited.add(node); for neighbor in graph[node]: if neighbor not in visited and dfs(neighbor, visited): return True; return False. Time: O(V+E).

DFS Pattern: Connected components / flood fill. DFS from unvisited node marks entire component. Modify grid in-place or use visited set. For islands: dfs turns '1's to '0's to avoid revisit. Count DFS calls = number of components. Time: O(m*n) for grid, O(V+E) for graph. DFS uses less memory than BFS for deep narrow structures.

DFS Pattern: Cycle detection in directed graph. Use three states: WHITE(unvisited), GRAY(in-stack), BLACK(done). If DFS reaches a GRAY node, cycle detected. Reset to BLACK when done. Template: if color[node]==GRAY: return True (cycle); color[node]=GRAY; for neighbor: if dfs(neighbor): return True; color[node]=BLACK; return False. Time: O(V+E).

DFS Pattern: Topological sort via DFS. Post-order DFS: add node to stack AFTER processing all descendants. Reverse of post-order = topological order. For each unvisited node, run DFS. If cycle detected, no valid topological order. Works for course schedule, build dependencies. Time: O(V+E). Compare: Kahn's BFS is iterative, DFS post-order is recursive.

DFS Pattern: Generate all permutations. Use DFS with backtracking. State: current permutation, remaining elements. At each step, try each remaining element. Template: def dfs(path, remaining): if not remaining: result.append(path[:]); return; for i,num in enumerate(remaining): path.append(num); dfs(path, remaining[:i]+remaining[i+1:]); path.pop(). Time: O(n! * n).

DFS Pattern: Generate all subsets (power set). Two choices for each element: include or exclude. Template: def dfs(index, current): result.append(current[:]); for i in range(index, len(nums)): current.append(nums[i]); dfs(i+1, current); current.pop(). Or: dfs(i, include) and dfs(i, exclude) binary choice. Time: O(2^n * n), Space: O(n) recursion.

DFS Pattern: Combination sum with backtracking. Find all combinations summing to target. Allow reuse (same index) or no reuse (index+1). Prune when current sum > target. Template: def dfs(index, remaining, path): if remaining==0: result.append(path[:]); return; for i in range(index, len(candidates)): if candidates[i]<=remaining: path.append(candidates[i]); dfs(i, remaining-candidates[i], path); path.pop(). Time: O(2^(t/min)).

DFS Pattern: N-Queens / constraint satisfaction. Place N queens on NxN board with no conflicts. DFS on rows, try each column. Prune if column, diagonal, or anti-diagonal already occupied. Use sets for O(1) conflict check. Template: def dfs(row): if row==n: result.append(board[:]); return; for col in range(n): if valid(row,col): place(row,col); dfs(row+1); remove(row,col). Time: O(n!).

DFS Pattern: Maze solving / path in grid. DFS explores until reaching exit or dead end. Mark current cell as visited, try all directions, unmark on backtrack. If finding all paths, collect path at destination. If finding any path, return True on first success. Time: O(4^(m*n)) worst case for all paths. Use BFS for shortest path.

DFS Pattern: Tree diameter. Longest path between any two nodes. DFS returns height from each node. At each node, diameter through it = left_height + right_height. Track global max. Template: def dfs(node): if not node: return 0; left=dfs(node.left); right=dfs(node.right); self.diameter=max(self.diameter, left+right); return 1+max(left,right). Time: O(n).

DFS Pattern: Lowest common ancestor (LCA). DFS finds if target nodes are in left/right subtrees. If node == p or q, return node. Recurse left and right. If both return non-null, current node is LCA. If only one side, propagate that result up. Time: O(n). For repeated queries, use binary lifting for O(log n) per query after O(n log n) preprocessing.

DFS Pattern: Serialize and deserialize binary tree. Preorder DFS serializes tree to string. Use '#' for null. Deserialize by consuming tokens: if '#' return None, else create node and recurse left then right. Use an iterator or index pointer to track position. Time: O(n). Alternative: level-order BFS serialization.

DFS Pattern: Number of paths in grid (counting). DFS with memoization counts paths from (0,0) to (m-1,n-1). States: (row, col). Transitions: move right or down (or all 4 directions if allowed). With obstacles: skip blocked cells. Equivalent to DP bottom-up. Use memoization to avoid recomputation. Time: O(m*n).

DFS Pattern: Word search in grid. Check if word exists in grid starting from any cell. DFS from each starting cell. Mark cell visited by temporarily changing value. Four-directional DFS. Restore cell after backtracking. Early termination if word is found. Time: O(m*n * 4^L) where L=word length. Trie optimization for multiple words.

DFS Pattern: Strongly connected components (Kosaraju's). Two-pass DFS. First pass: DFS on original graph, push nodes to stack in finish order. Second pass: DFS on reversed graph in stack order. Each DFS tree in second pass is one SCC. Time: O(V+E). Alternative: Tarjan's single-pass DFS using low-link values.

DFS Pattern: Tarjan's algorithm for bridges and articulation points. Track discovery time and low value. Low[v] = min discovery time reachable via back edges. Bridge: edge (u,v) where low[v] > disc[u]. Articulation point: u is root with 2+ children, or non-root with child v where low[v] >= disc[u]. Time: O(V+E).

DFS Pattern: Expression tree evaluation. Build expression tree where leaves are operands and internal nodes are operators. DFS evaluates: if leaf, return value; else evaluate left and right subtrees, apply operator. Time: O(n). Use for: evaluate math expressions, parse and compute nested structures, AST interpretation.

DFS Pattern: Course schedule / prerequisite check. Model as directed graph. DFS detects cycle (impossible schedule) or performs topological sort (valid order). For 'can finish': just detect cycle. For 'find order': return topo sort. Template uses GRAY/BLACK coloring for cycle detection during DFS. Time: O(V+E) where V=courses, E=prerequisites.

DFS Pattern: Binary tree maximum path sum. Path can start and end at any node. DFS returns max gain from a node downward (only one direction). At each node: path_through = node.val + max(0, left_gain) + max(0, right_gain). Update global max. Return node.val + max(0, left_gain, right_gain) to parent. Time: O(n).
